text: The surgeon opens the peritoneum at the Douglas pouch, followed by dissection of the rectovaginal space and the anterior part of the rectum. A lateral dissection on the left side of the rectum reveals both sides of the muscle defect in the pelvic floor, providing a comprehensive view of the dissected muscle defect.
The surgeon performs a peritoneal incision at the sacral promontory, followed by dissection of the retrorectal space to access the pelvic floor, joining the dissections from previous steps.
The surgeon performs a digital rectal examination, visually revealing the presence of a perineal hernia followed by the examination of the rectum.

The surgeon places a biological mesh over the muscle defect, securing it with interrupted Vicryl IIO sutures to the lateral rectal wall and pelvic floor. The proximal end of the mesh is fixed to the sacral promontory using two Vicryl IIO sutures, completing the posterior rectopexy.
The surgeon closes the peritoneum using two running barbed sutures of PDS-III-O, completing the final step of the hernia repair procedure.
